questions.q: What is RoxTrack?
questions.a: RoxTrack is a Garmin Connect IQ app designed specifically for HYROX races. It tracks your time, heart rate, and distance across all 8 running segments and 8 workout stations, giving you real-time performance data on your wrist.

questions.q: Which Garmin devices are supported?
questions.a: RoxTrack supports a wide range of Garmin watches — from the Fenix 6 series all the way through the latest Fenix 8, Forerunner 970, and Venu 4. It also works on touchscreen watches like the Vivoactive 5/6, Venu Sq 2, and Forerunner 165. In total, 50+ devices are supported including the Epix 2, Forerunner 245/255/265/745/945/955/965, and more. Check the Connect IQ store listing for the full compatibility list.

questions.q: Is RoxTrack free?
questions.a: Yes, RoxTrack is completely free! If you find it useful and want to support ongoing development, you can buy me a coffee. Donations are always appreciated but never required.

questions.q: What languages does RoxTrack support?
questions.a: RoxTrack is available in 10 languages: English, German, French, Italian, Spanish, Portuguese, Dutch, Polish, and Swedish. The app will automatically use your watch's language setting if it's one of these.

questions.q: Can I use RoxTrack for training, not just race day?
questions.a: Absolutely. You can scale the standard HYROX race down to 25%, 50%, or 75% using the Race Distance setting, which is perfect for practice sessions. Or use Custom Workouts to build your own training routines — including timed intervals for EMOM, Tabata-style, and circuit timer workouts.

questions.q: What does Race Distance do?
questions.a: Race Distance lets you scale the workout to 25%, 50%, 75%, or 100% of a full HYROX race. All station targets and run distances adjust proportionally. This is great for training sessions where you want to practice the format without doing the full distance.

questions.q: How do I set a Run Pace Target?
questions.a: Select Run Pace Target from the settings menu. You'll pick a minute value first, then seconds — giving you 1-second precision from 3:30/km up to 8:00/km. During the race, your pace display will be color-coded — green when you're on or ahead of your target, red when you're slower.

questions.q: What does Goal Time do?
questions.a: Goal Time lets you set a target finish time for your race, from 1 minute up to 3 hours. During the race, the timer will be color-coded: green when you're ahead of pace, orange when you're close (90%+), and red when you've exceeded your goal. Set it to OFF if you don't want goal tracking.

questions.q: What are the Run Mode options?
questions.a: Run Mode controls how RoxTrack tracks your running segments. Outdoor uses GPS for real distance and pace. Indoor disables GPS and uses the watch's accelerometer to estimate distance and pace. Treadmill is optimized for treadmill running. Cardio uses Garmin's Cardio activity instead of a run profile, so distance and pace aren't tracked — run segments display like stations instead.

questions.q: What is the RoxZone setting?
questions.a: RoxZone adds transition segments between your runs and stations, just like the actual RoxZone areas in a HYROX race. When ON, these segments are tracked separately so you can see how long your transitions take. Turn it OFF if you want to go directly from station to run without tracking transitions.

questions.q: What does the Touch setting do?
questions.a: Touch is OFF by default to prevent accidental screen taps during a race. When you turn it ON, tapping the screen works the same as pressing the Lap button to advance to the next segment. This can be useful on touchscreen watches if you prefer tapping over pressing a physical button.

questions.q: Are my settings saved if I close the app?
questions.a: Yes. All your settings — Race Distance, Run Pace Target, Goal Time, Run Mode, RoxZone, and Touch — are automatically saved every time you change them. They persist through app restarts, discards, and even crashes. You only need to set them once.

questions.q: How do I start a race?
questions.a: Open RoxTrack on your watch and configure your settings (race distance, run mode, pace target, etc.). Then select Start Warmup. When you're ready to begin, press the Lap button to exit the warmup and start your first running segment. Your race timer begins at that point.

questions.q: Do I have to set my settings each time?
questions.a: No. All your settings are automatically saved every time you change them and persist through app restarts, discards, and even crashes. You only need to configure them once — on future races, just jump straight to Start Warm-Up for a quick start.

questions.q: Does the warmup count toward my race time?
questions.a: No. The warmup is a separate phase that runs before the race starts. Your total race time begins when you advance out of the warmup into the first segment. Warmup time is not included in your final results.

questions.q: How do I advance to the next segment?
questions.a: Press the Lap button on your watch to advance to the next segment. RoxTrack uses manual progression only — there's no auto-lap — so you're always in control of when transitions happen.

questions.q: What if I press Lap too early?
questions.a: No worries! Press the Down button to trigger the undo flow, which will reverse you back to the previous segment. Your time and distance are rewound so your data stays accurate.

questions.q: What data is shown during running segments?
questions.a: During running segments, RoxTrack displays your current pace, elapsed distance for that segment, segment time, and heart rate.

questions.q: What data is shown during workout stations?
questions.a: During station segments, you'll see the station name, the target reps or meters, your elapsed time for that station, and your heart rate.

questions.q: Can I create custom workouts?
questions.a: Yes! RoxTrack supports custom workout creation directly on the watch. You can build reps/distance-based workouts or timed interval workouts, give them a custom name, and save them for reuse. Please note that custom workouts are not supported on all devices — older or lower-memory watches may not have this feature available.

questions.q: What's the difference between standard and timed workouts?
questions.a: In standard mode, each exercise has its own distance or rep target and you press Lap to advance when you're done. In timed mode, you set a global work time (e.g. 1 minute) and every exercise runs for that duration with a countdown timer that auto-advances to the next segment when time is up. Timed mode is ideal for EMOM, Tabata-style, and circuit timer workouts.

questions.q: How does the countdown timer work in timed workouts?
questions.a: During timed workouts, a countdown timer is displayed showing the remaining time for each exercise. When the timer reaches zero, the watch vibrates and automatically advances to the next segment — no need to press any buttons. You can still press Lap early to advance manually if you finish before time is up.

questions.q: What work times can I set for timed workouts?
questions.a: Work time ranges from 30 seconds to 10 minutes, in 30-second increments. This time applies to all exercises in the workout — each one gets the same duration per round.

questions.q: What exercises are available for custom workouts?
questions.a: You can choose from all the standard HYROX exercises: Run, SkiErg, Sled Push, Sled Pull, Burpee Broad Jumps, Row, Farmers Carry, Walking Lunges, Sandbag Lunges, and Wall Balls. In standard mode, there's also a Rest option you can add between exercises as a timed rest period.

questions.q: How do I set distances, reps, and rest times?
questions.a: In standard mode, after selecting an exercise you'll pick a target value. Most exercises use distance in meters (e.g. 50m–2000m for runs and rows, 5m–200m for sled pushes). Wall Balls use reps (5–200), and Rest uses time (15 seconds up to 5 minutes). Each exercise has its own range and increment size.

questions.q: Can I set a transition time between exercises?
questions.a: Yes! You can set a transition time that gets added between exercises. Options range from 30 seconds to 5 minutes, or you can turn it off entirely. There's also a Flexible option — this adds a transition segment that you advance manually with the Lap button, rather than a fixed countdown. Great for when your rest varies.

questions.q: Can I add rest between rounds?
questions.a: Yes — in timed workouts, you can set a Rest After Round time from 30 seconds to 5 minutes. This inserts an auto-advancing rest segment at the end of each round (except the final round), giving you recovery time before the next circuit begins.

questions.q: How do rounds work?
questions.a: You can set your workout to repeat for 1 to 10 rounds. Each round repeats the full sequence of exercises you've built. For example, if you create a workout with Run → SkiErg → Sled Push and set it to 3 rounds, you'll cycle through all three exercises three times.

questions.q: Can I edit a workout after creating it?
questions.a: Yes, you can fully edit any saved workout. From the review screen you can change targets on individual exercises, delete exercises, add new ones, reorder the exercise sequence, adjust the round count, and change the transition time. You can also rename or delete entire workouts from the workout list.

questions.q: Can I duplicate a workout?
questions.a: Yes. From the workout list, select a workout and choose Duplicate. This creates a full copy with all exercises, targets, and settings intact, named with "Copy" appended. You can then edit the copy without affecting the original — useful for creating variations of a workout.

questions.q: Can I name my custom workouts?
questions.a: Yes! When saving a custom workout, you can give it a custom name using the on-watch text picker. You can also rename workouts later from the workout list. If another workout already has the same name, RoxTrack will automatically add a number to keep names unique. On devices that don't support the text picker, workouts are automatically named (e.g. "Workout 1", "Workout 2").

questions.q: Can I reorder exercises in my workout?
questions.a: Yes. From the review screen, select Reorder and pick the exercise you want to move. Then use the Up and Down buttons to shift it into the right position and press Start to confirm.

questions.q: Why can't I see the custom workout option on my watch?
questions.a: Custom workouts require additional memory and are not available on all supported devices. Some older or entry-level watches may not have enough resources to run this feature. If you don't see the option, your device may not be compatible with custom workouts — but the core HYROX race tracking works on all supported watches.

questions.q: How many custom workouts can I save?
questions.a: You can save up to 10 custom workouts on your device.

questions.q: Where can I see my race history?
questions.a: RoxTrack saves your last 10 races on-device. You can access them from the History menu within the app. Each entry shows your total time and per-segment breakdowns.

questions.q: What does the summary screen show?
questions.a: After completing a race or workout, the summary screen shows your overall totals — total time, run time, station time, and RoxZone time. Press Down to scroll into a detailed per-segment breakdown showing the time for every individual segment. Press Up to scroll back to the totals.

questions.q: Does RoxTrack save to Garmin Connect?
questions.a: Yes! Each race is saved as a standard FIT activity file, which syncs automatically to Garmin Connect. You'll see individual laps for each segment in your activity details.

questions.q: Can I export my race data?
questions.a: Your race data is saved as a FIT file in Garmin Connect, which you can export and analyze in any compatible platform like Strava, TrainingPeaks, or WKO.

questions.q: Can I compare my race results?
questions.a: Open History, select a race, choose Compare, and pick another race to view them side by side with per-segment differences. To compare results, both races must use the same setup (for example, same distance and RoxZone settings).

questions.q: The app crashed during my race. Is my data lost?
questions.a: If a crash occurs, any laps already recorded will be saved in the FIT file up to that point. The current in-progress segment may be lost. We recommend ensuring your watch firmware and the app are both up to date.

questions.q: Does RoxTrack drain my battery?
questions.a: Battery impact depends on your Run Mode. Outdoor mode uses GPS, which draws the most power — similar to any GPS activity on your watch. Indoor, Treadmill, and Cardio modes don't use GPS and have a much smaller battery impact. For a typical HYROX race (1–2 hours), battery drain should be very manageable on any supported device.

questions.q: My heart rate data looks wrong. What can I do?
questions.a: Heart rate accuracy depends on your watch sensor. For best results during HYROX, wear the watch snugly above the wrist bone. For maximum accuracy, consider pairing an external chest strap HR monitor via ANT+ or Bluetooth.

questions.q: How do I update RoxTrack?
questions.a: Updates are delivered through the Garmin Connect IQ Store. Open the Connect IQ app on your phone, go to My Apps, and check for available updates. We recommend keeping auto-updates enabled.
